title: bromus rigens subsp. macrantherus
intro: Bromus rigens subsp. macrantherus, also known as big bluegrass or giant wildrye, is a perennial grass species native to the western United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, and is valued for its ability to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The health benefits of Bromus rigens subsp. macrantherus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
